entries.d: To have bad luck
entries.s: She was very _____ today and missed the bus by just one single minute — she saw it driving away.

entries.d: To think of something you really want and hope it happens
entries.s: She closed her eyes tightly and _____ silently in her head before blowing out all twelve candles on her birthday cake.

entries.d: To want something very much and hope to get it
entries.s: What did you secretly _____ for when you blew out the candles on your birthday cake?

entries.d: To cause good or bad things to happen
entries.s: Some superstitious people think that seeing a black cat crossing your path _____ bad luck to you.

entries.d: When a wish or dream really happens
entries.s: I really hope my biggest dream of visiting Japan next year will _____ one day soon when I save enough money.

entries.d: A bit scary and strange, like ghosts
entries.s: The old abandoned house on the dark hill at night looks really _____ with broken windows and creaking doors.

entries.d: When good or bad things happen to you by chance
entries.s: I _____ really good luck today — I found a €5 note on the street on my way to school!

entries.d: To think that old beliefs about luck are true
entries.s: Do you really _____ in silly superstitions like walking under ladders being bad, or are you just joking?

entries.d: A device next to your bed that makes a noise to wake you up
entries.s: My loud ringing _____ didn't work this morning at 7 am, so I overslept and was late for the school bus.

entries.d: A question to ask if someone was successful
entries.s: Did you finally find your lost house keys under the sofa? _____

entries.d: Next to, at the side of
entries.s: She sat down quietly _____ me on the sofa so close that our shoulders touched.

entries.d: A polite way to ask if something is OK
entries.s: _____ if I open the big window? It's very hot in here and I need fresh air to cool down.

entries.d: Very bad, wanting to hurt others
entries.s: In the classic fairy tale, the _____ queen wanted to poison the beautiful young princess with a red apple.

entries.d: A way to tell someone you are not being serious
entries.s: Don't worry and don't be upset, _____! I didn't really eat your last chocolate bar — it's in the fridge.

entries.d: To act as if someone or something is not there
entries.s: She was very angry at her little brother and decided to _____ him all day by not talking or looking at him.

entries.d: Happy with what you have or what happened
entries.s: The teacher was very _____ with my excellent test results — I got 95% and she gave me a big smile.

entries.d: To shout very loudly, often because you are scared
entries.s: She _____ out loud in fright when she saw the huge black spider crawling on the kitchen wall.

entries.d: A warm cover you lie in when camping outdoors
entries.s: Don't forget to bring your warm _____ for the overnight school trip — we'll sleep inside them in the tent.

entries.d: A ghost or the soul of a dead person
entries.s: The scared children told ghost stories about a white _____ that lives in the old haunted castle on the hill.

entries.d: A belief that certain things bring good or bad luck
entries.s: The silly idea that the number 13 is unlucky is just a _____ — there's no real proof that it causes bad luck.

entries.d: To make someone or something come closer
entries.s: The bright colourful flowers in our garden _____ many honey bees and butterflies during the warm summer months.

entries.d: To go into a place
entries.s: Please take off your dirty shoes at the door before you _____ the clean house — it's our family rule.

entries.d: When your hair is cut shorter by a hairdresser
entries.s: You look very different today — did you just get a new _____ at the hair salon? It's much shorter now.

entries.d: Easy to see or understand
entries.s: It was absolutely _____ that she was really tired because she kept yawning and rubbing her eyes in class.

entries.d: Done in the same way for a long time
entries.s: On Christmas Day, we always eat a _____ turkey dinner with the whole family, just like my grandparents did.

entries.d: To make someone believe something that is not true
entries.s: My clever brother _____ me into eating a really hot chilli yesterday by telling me it was a sweet pepper.

entries.d: Having or bringing bad luck
entries.s: Many people think that the number 13 is _____ and avoid choosing hotel rooms or flights with that number.

entries.d: To make a high sound by blowing air through your lips
entries.s: He always _____ his favourite pop song while walking happily to school in the morning with his lips.

entries.d: A thin line or opening in a surface
entries.s: There is a big zigzag _____ in the old plaster wall of our house that needs to be fixed by a builder soon.

entries.d: A small grey forest bird known for its repeated two-syllable call in spring.
entries.s: We could clearly hear a _____ calling its name over and over from the tall green forest trees on our spring walk.

entries.d: The hard path next to a road where people walk
entries.s: Walk on the _____ next to the buildings, not on the busy road — it's much safer for pedestrians.

entries.d: Having a lot of money
entries.s: The _____ businessman bought a huge mansion with 20 rooms, a swimming pool, and three sports cars.

entries.d: To move something quickly from side to side or up and down
entries.s: _____ the juice bottle well with your hand up and down before you drink to mix the pulp at the bottom.

entries.d: Believing that certain things bring good or bad luck
entries.s: My elderly grandmother is very _____ and refuses to walk under a ladder or break a mirror because of bad luck.

entries.d: To get ill with a runny nose and cough
entries.s: Wear a thick warm jacket in the snow or you might _____ and be sick in bed for a week with a runny nose.

entries.d: A small brush you use to clean your teeth
entries.s: I need a new _____ — the old one I use to clean my teeth every morning is too worn out with bent bristles.

entries.d: To plan or organise something
entries.s: Let's _____ a meeting for next Tuesday after school at 4 pm in the library to work on our project.

entries.d: A way to say you are certain about something
entries.s: Will the train come soon? — _____. It's always exactly on time at 3:15 pm every day without fail.

entries.d: An object that you think brings you good luck
entries.s: She always carries her small _____ — a tiny silver horseshoe — in her pocket during every school exam for good luck.

entries.d: White powder you put on food to give it more taste
entries.s: Can you please pass me the _____ shaker, please? My vegetable soup tastes a bit too bland and needs seasoning.

entries.d: Really, not joking
entries.s: Are you _____ going to try to eat all five big burgers by yourself? That's way too much food for one person!
